readiness_reasons: Missing segment-level revenue and profit splits from filings; all segment models are analytical
Total shares outstanding unknown, preventing EPS and per-share valuation
SST/AIDC business is pre-revenue; all financial estimates are speculative
No Q2 2026 order backlog or delivery schedule available
Required consolidated three-year forecast lines are incomplete.
Material segment three-year driver lines are missing: PV Inverters, SST/AIDC Power
Valuation has not closed from mutually exclusive buckets to per-share fair value.
project_backlog_delivery driver chain is incomplete; missing: opening backlog, new orders, ending backlog
Three-year values remain missing for consolidated line(s): EPS, operating_profit

company_model.business_archetype: Clean energy power electronics equipment manufacturer and energy storage system integrator
company_model.value_proposition_and_customers: Provides solar inverters (string and central) and utility-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) worldwide, along with solid-state transformers for AI data centers. Serves utility companies, commercial & industrial clients, and data center operators.
company_model.revenue_equation: Revenue = Project delivery (GWh) × Average system price (CNY/Wh) + Inverter shipments (GW) × ASP (CNY/W) + emerging SST/AIDC revenue.
company_model.profit_equation: Gross profit = Revenue - battery cost - inverter components - installation costs; Operating profit = Gross profit - R&D - SG&A; Net profit after finance cost (interest & FX) and tax.
company_model.cash_flow_equation: OCF = Net profit + depreciation ± working capital changes; Capex for factory and R&D; FCF = OCF - capex.
company_model.capital_intensity_and_reinvestment: High R&D (¥4.175B in FY2025) and overseas capacity expansion (Poland factory 2027H1). Working capital heavily influenced by contract liabilities (¥122B) and prepayments.
share_count_cross_checks: Could be derived from market cap / share price or total_share from filings, but neither is available in the evidence payload
moat_mechanisms: Brand and bankability with global utility customers
Technology leadership in inverters (high efficiency) and ESS system integration
R&D scale (41.75B CNY in FY2025) and large installed base
Global service network and emerging local manufacturing (Poland plant)
First-mover advantage in SST/AIDC power electronics
moat_to_financial_transmission: Brand enables premium pricing and margin resilience
R&D drives product differentiation and cost reduction
Installed base leads to repeat orders and service revenue
Local manufacturing mitigates tariff risks and strengthens competitive position
structural_risks: US policy risk (38.4% tariff, potential FCC ban on inverters with communication functions)
Intense price competition, especially in China PV inverter market with industry overcapacity
Battery cost volatility and supply chain concentration
Technology risk: SST adoption still unproven, long customer qualification cycles
Working capital strain from large project orders and slow cash conversion (OCF/net profit only 0.53x Q1 2026)
key_unknowns: FY2026 full-year revenue recovery after weak Q1
European ESS market sustainability at 60% CAGR
SST product revenue ramp and margin profile post July 2026 launch
US regulatory outcome for inverters and energy storage

business_unit_map.economic_role: Solar inverter sales; product sale with some project attachment
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Gross profit = Revenue - cost of materials (semiconductors, magnetics) - manufacturing; Op profit after R&D/S&M allocation
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Working capital from inventory and receivables; capex for production lines

business_unit_map.economic_role: BESS system integration and project delivery
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Gross profit = Revenue - battery procurement - BMS/PCS - installation; Op profit after R&D and S&M
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Large contract liabilities (¥122B) and prepayments; capex for factory (Poland) and R&D

business_unit_map.economic_role: Solid-state transformers and power electronics for AI data centers; pre-revenue
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Revenue = SST units × ASP, initially zero
business_unit_map.profit_driver_equation: Expected high gross margin once commercial; heavy R&D expense in early stages
business_unit_map.cash_and_capital_equation: Negative cash flow due to R&D; financed by parent operations

business_unit_map.economic_role: Electrolyzers for green hydrogen; minimal current contribution
business_unit_map.revenue_driver_equation: Revenue = Electrolyzer shipments × ASP; not material to consolidated
reported_metrics: Orders described as 'good' in investor Q&A
business_unit_map.next_verification: Monitor for materiality in future filings

segment_models.products_or_services: Utility-scale and C&I energy storage systems, including PCS, BMS, EMS, and full system integration.
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Direct sales to utilities, developers, and large C&I customers globally, especially Europe, US, and China.
demand_and_order_drivers: European energy transition and policy support
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Expanding with new factories (Poland 2027H1); competition from CATL, BYD, Fluence, Tesla. Chinese overcapacity partly, but international local content rules create moats.
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Company maintains leading global market share in ESS integration. Q1 2026 shipments flat YoY, but order intake strong (contract liabilities 122B CNY).
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: ASP under pressure from declining battery costs, but system price supported by longer duration and services. Unit profit 0.11 CNY/Wh in Q1 2026.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Main cost battery cells; LFP costs stabilizing but may face tariff impacts. Prepayments 40B CNY indicate securing supply.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Gross margin 30% in Q1 2026; improvement from regional mix (higher-margin Europe). Operating leverage positive as volume grows.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: High contract liabilities (122B) indicate advance payments; but receivables high; OCF/net profit ratio only 0.53 in Q1, indicating cash conversion lag.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Demand robust, European 3-year CAGR 60%, AIDC orders starting H2 2026. Supply expanding but company's Poland plant strengthens local position. Margins improving as cost pass-through in less competitive markets occurs. Working capital will improve as large projects convert to cash.
segment_models.valuation_treatment: Core business, valued on PE with growth premium
strongest_counterevidence: US tariff (38.4%) and potential FCC ban on inverters
Q1 consolidated revenue decline -82.55% YoY suggesting demand/timing risk
missing_inputs: Exact segment revenue and profit split from filings
Q2 2026 order backlog and delivery schedule
segment_models.next_verification: Half-year 2026 filing for revenue segmentation and margin update

segment_models.products_or_services: String and central inverters for residential, commercial, and utility PV.
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Global distribution partners, EPCs, and direct project sales.
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Severe overcapacity among Chinese competitors; intense price competition.
segment_models.volume_share_utilization: Company among top players but facing pricing pressure. Shipments likely declined in Q1 2026.
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: ASP declining due to commoditization; differentiation via efficiency and reliability.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: Component costs (IGBT, SiC) may increase; some cost reduction from scale.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Gross margin likely 25-30%, under pressure from competitive pricing.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Standard working capital; receivables tied to project invoicing.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Demand steady but competitive. Margins compressed. US policy risk overhang could further impact.
strongest_counterevidence: US potential ban on inverters with communication functions
Q1 2026 revenue decline possibly due to inverter segment weakness
missing_inputs: Exact revenue and margin from filings

segment_models.products_or_services: Solid-state transformers (SST) for data center power distribution, AIDC power electronics.
segment_models.customers_and_channel: Direct to data center operators and via partnerships.
demand_and_order_drivers: AIDC power shortages and grid bottlenecks
segment_models.industry_supply_and_capacity: Emerging; few competitors with product ready. SunGrow first mover with 10kV SST on July 9, 2026.
segment_models.price_asp_take_rate: Premium pricing expected as first entrant.
segment_models.unit_cost_and_input_prices: High cost due to SiC devices; cost will decline with volume and iterations.
segment_models.margin_and_operating_leverage: Expected high gross margin once commercial; heavy R&D expense initially.
segment_models.working_capital_and_cash_conversion: Negative initially due to R&D; cash conversion uncertain.
segment_models.causal_analysis: Technology commercialization milestone approaching. If successful, large TAM but adoption uncertain. R&D costs high (need 300+ personnel, ¥500M/year). Company claims multi-sector applications to spread R&D.

underwriting_questions.question: Can the European ESS market sustain 60% CAGR and deliver high-margin orders through 2028?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: European ESS is the primary margin driver; a slowdown or pricing pressure would sharply reduce consolidated earnings. A 60% CAGR assumption underpins bull/base revenue forecasts and segment margins above 30%.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unresolved; channel checks claim 60% 3-year CAGR, but no official filings confirm. Q1 2026 overall revenue declined sharply, creating doubt.
bull_evidence: KPE03: 欧洲储能3年复合增速上修至60%
KPE04: 近期欧洲光储展会斩获较多订单，预计明年欧洲储能增速由40-50%上修至60%
bear_evidence: Q1 2026 consolidated revenue -82.55% YoY, possibly indicating ESS deliveries delayed or weak demand
EU financing restrictions affecting ~20% of projects, though company exposure claimed small
missing_evidence: Official company guidance on European ESS growth and order book
underwriting_questions.next_verification: SunGrow H1 2026 results and any public order announcements

underwriting_questions.question: Will SunGrow convert its massive contract liabilities (122B CNY) into cash profit with acceptable margin, or will working capital drag and cost overruns erode equity value?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Contract liabilities are a leading indicator of future revenue, but their conversion to cash matters. High receivables and inventory ratios (40% and 44% of annualized revenue) in Q1 suggest working capital drain. Failure to collect and deliver profitably would reduce FCF and ROIC.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unresolved; OCF/net profit only 0.53x in Q1 2026, indicating poor cash conversion.
bull_evidence: Company says prepayments (40B) are to lock in supply, implying strong future delivery plans
Historical OCF was strong in FY2025 (16918 mn)
bear_evidence: Finance expense ratio +2.42pp YoY, indicating funding cost pressure
missing_evidence: Aging of receivables and contract liabilities
Delivery schedule for the 122B contract liabilities
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Q2 2026 cash flow statement and management discussion

underwriting_questions.question: Are US regulatory risks for inverters and storage material enough to impair ~20% revenue exposure, or will safe-harbor provisions and local production (Poland) sufficiently insulate earnings?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: US market accounts for ~20% of revenue and an estimated ¥25-30B in annual profit. A ban on inverters or stricter storage tariffs could remove a high-margin segment and reduce EPS significantly.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Risk probability disputed; company claims products are compliant and safe-harbor extends to 2028H1; Goldman sees risk, CITIC sees low probability.
bull_evidence: KPE07: FCC主要管辖通信设备，直接限制概率较低，产品无通讯功能
Company states US projects until 28H1 within safe harbor
bear_evidence: KPE01/KPE02: 管理层电话会议重点——关于美国禁令草案报道，维持中性评级
underwriting_questions.next_verification: FCC/FERC announcements and company's product certification updates

underwriting_questions.question: What is the realistic revenue and profit contribution from AIDC storage and SST power beyond 2027, and can SunGrow's first-mover advantage translate into durable moats and high ROIC?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: These second-curve businesses are expected to contribute significantly to terminal value. If they fail to scale or margins disappoint, the option value collapses.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Speculative; SST product launch imminent but no orders yet. AIDC storage has small orders (hundreds of MWh) but no large-scale contracts.
bull_evidence: KPE05: AIDC储能已有订单（数百MWh），英伟达10项测试标准利好头部厂商
KPE06: AI数据中心电力缺口催生表后供电市场，储能成刚需
KPE03: SST 7月9日发布商业化产品，客户评价高
bear_evidence: No formal revenue or profit disclosed
Competition from Fluence, Tesla, and traditional transformer makers
SiC supply chain concentration and potential bottlenecks
underwriting_questions.next_verification: July 9 SST launch and subsequent H2 2026 order announcements

underwriting_questions.question: Can the solar inverter business maintain profitability while facing severe pricing pressure and a potential US ban, or will it become a drag on consolidated returns?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Inverters still contribute meaningful volume, and a margin collapse or loss of US market could offset ESS growth gains.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Margins likely under pressure; no separate disclosure. US risk overhang.
bull_evidence: Company differentiates through high efficiency and reliability
bear_evidence: Potential US ban could remove entire US inverter revenue
underwriting_questions.next_verification: H1 2026 segment reporting or management commentary on inverter trends

forecast_lines.formula: Base assumption: FY2026 -10% decline due to weak Q1, recovery in H2 from ESS deliveries; FY2027 +25% driven by European ESS 60% growth and AIDC initial ramp; FY2028 +15% continued growth.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: European ESS demand realization and AIDC order conversion

forecast_lines.formula: Q1 2026 gross margin 33.26%, but full-year pressure from mix and tariffs. Assumes gradual improvement as higher-margin European ESS share increases.
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Regional mix and battery cost pass-through

forecast_lines.formula: FY2025 estimated from net profit and finance cost; Q1 2026 17.1% but full-year weak top-line leverage. Gradual recovery as revenue scale improves.
Net profit = Revenue * Operating Margin * (1 - tax rate) adjusted for finance costs. Tax rate assumed 15%.
Parent net profit / diluted share count
Assumes OCF/net profit ratio improves from 0.52x in Q1 2026 to 0.82x by 2028 as working capital normalizes.
Estimated for Poland factory completion, R&D equipment. FY2025 figure not available in evidence.
No disclosed baseline; KPE suggests 2027E 80GWh in bull scenario
Assumes slight improvement as battery costs stabilize and higher-margin regions grow
forecast_lines.key_sensitivity: Battery procurement cost and regional mix

operating_assumptions: Europe ESS CAGR 50% (slightly below private estimates of 60%)
AIDC storage orders start H2 2026, contribute modestly in 2027
SST product launch successful, first orders late 2027
No new US inverter ban; existing 38.4% tariff remains
Gross margin ~31.5% in 2026, improving to 32.5% by 2028
scenarios.falsification: European orders growth below 30% in 2027H1; SST customer rejections; US policy worsens
missing_inputs: EPS due to unknown share count

operating_assumptions: Europe ESS CAGR 60% materializes; Poland factory accelerates market share
AIDC storage orders ramp to >5 GWh by 2027
SST achieves >¥2B revenue by 2028 with >40% gross margin
No US ban, tariffs not increased
scenarios.valuation_method: PE on 2028E net profit, plus option value for AIDC
scenarios.falsification: SST product fails customer qualification; US bans inverters

operating_assumptions: SST launch delayed or fails to gain traction
US inverter ban implemented, wiping out US inverter profit
Gross margin falls to 28% due to pricing pressure
scenarios.falsification: New US policy not enacted; European orders recover

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: European ESS market growth drives revenue and margin expansion
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Europe ESS Revenue = Previous year × (1 + growth rate)
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Could justify 20-22x PE vs 15x in bear

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: AIDC energy storage and SST create new growth optionality
thesis_financial_bridges.operating_driver: AIDC storage GWh and SST revenue
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: AIDC profit = GWh × unit profit; SST profit = Revenue × 20% net margin
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: AIDC: 2 GWh in 2027 at ¥0.15/Wh; SST: ¥0.5B revenue in 2028
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: AIDC: 10 GWh in 2027; SST: ¥3B revenue in 2028
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Option value of ¥6000-¥12000 per share (market cap not per share) in SOTP

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: US policy risk undercuts high-margin revenue
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: US profit contribution = US revenue × segment margin
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: No ban; US revenue ~20% of total, margin 35%
thesis_financial_bridges.bull_assumption: Ban avoided and tariffs reduced; US revenue grows 10%
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: Inverter banned, storage tariff raised to 50%; US revenue halves
thesis_financial_bridges.revenue_impact: Bear case reduces total revenue by ~10%
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: Bear case reduces net profit by ¥3-4B
thesis_financial_bridges.fcf_or_capital_impact: Lower working capital and potential stranded assets
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Bear case would compress PE to 12-15x
missing_inputs: Precise US revenue and profit split

thesis_financial_bridges.thesis_or_question: Working capital normalization releases cash flow
thesis_financial_bridges.driver_formula: Free cash flow = Net profit × OCF conversion - Capex
thesis_financial_bridges.base_assumption: OCF/NI improves from 0.65x in 2026 to 0.85x in 2028
thesis_financial_bridges.bear_assumption: Receivables build up, ratio stays at 0.5x
thesis_financial_bridges.profit_impact: No direct profit impact, but may cause impairments
thesis_financial_bridges.valuation_impact: Higher FCF supports higher PER and dividend capacity

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Brand and bankability with global utilities
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Repeat order rate and average project size with top 10 utility customers over 3 years
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: versus Fluence, Tesla, and domestic Chinese competitors
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: No quantitative data; company claims strong repeat orders, but unverified
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Higher win rate -> market share; premium pricing -> gross margin
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Q1 2026 revenue decline of -82.55% YoY raises doubt on order continuity
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Gather utility customer lists and project awards from EIA/BNEF

moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: R&D intensity (R&D/revenue) vs peers; product efficiency and grid code compliance certifications
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: vs Sungrow's own history and vs Huawei, SMA, Fluence
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: Sungrow spent ¥4.175B in 2025 (4.7% of revenue), higher than most domestic peers; efficiency and bankability data not directly supplied
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Product differentiation -> margin premium and market share retention
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Commoditization of PV inverters suggests R&D does not fully protect margins
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Compare patent filings and bankability surveys (e.g., BNEF)

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Local manufacturing (Poland plant) as tariff hedge
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Poland factory capacity utilization and revenue served locally vs export
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: Plant still under construction (2027H1), no output yet
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Avoids tariffs -> higher net pricing in Europe; qualifies for local content incentives
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Competitors may also build local plants; EU CBAM and other rules could still restrict

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: P/E on 2027E base scenario net profit
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: This bucket captures the entire existing business; no other bucket should use the same net profit stream.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: DCF of projected net profit in 2030, discounted at 10%
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: No overlap with core, as AIDC storage is incremental beyond 2027E base forecast.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: Scenario-weighted equity value (probability 30% bull scenario ¥1200B enterprise value, etc.)
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: No overlap; SST revenue not in core base case

valuation_closure.formula: fair_value = (core equity value + probability-weighted option values) / diluted shares; shares not available
valuation_closure.rating_consistency_check: Rating-free; no Buy/Hold/Sell. Valuation buckets mapped to scenarios, but per-share outputs are pending share count resolution.
double_counting_checks: Core bucket uses consolidated 2027E net profit, which excludes AIDC and SST option profiles
AIDC storage option valued on standalone incremental profit
SST option valued on standalone potential

Segment revenue and profit splits (no filing segmentation)

reconciliation_checks: Gross margin vs operating margin divergence: Q1 2026 op margin -6.94pp YoY despite gross margin -1.87pp, due to higher R&D/SG&A and finance costs. Flagged for monitoring.
Q1 2026 revenue decline -82.55% vs FY2025 run-rate, but annual forecast implies strong H2 recovery; this is a key variance to resolve with Q2 data.
Sell-side estimates (KPE05) imply 2027 net profit of ¥18B, well above our base case of ¥14.5B; divergence stems from aggressive AIDC and ESS assumptions.
Valuation bucket core equity value of ¥261B is close to current market cap of ¥258B, implying no margin of safety in base case.
analyst_instructions: Do not produce a Buy/Hold/Sell rating. All downstream modules use this packet as a shared model to challenge and update.
Freeze the reported facts in handoff_manifest. Override them only with a new filing evidence id and explain old/new assumption + recalculated EPS/FCF impact.
Any change to scenario probabilities must provide evidence and re-weight the three explicit scenarios; probabilities must sum to 100%.
Every moat claim must be mapped to an observable test and financial transmission; management assertions without supporting public data are labeled 'unproven'.
Segment revenue and margin splits are analytical; do not assign artificial revenue to units without a reporting basis.
EPS and per-share values remain blocked until diluted shares are sourced from Tushare total_share, market cap/price, or filing text.
Hydrogen equipment is tracked as an analytical unit with minimal data; promote to a material segment only if filings provide revenue, margin, and competitive position.
SST launch on July 9 is a key verification gate; downstream agents must update the SST bucket and scenario probabilities based on actual product specs and initial orders.
preprocessing_notes: Filings indicate FY2025 revenue 891.84B and Q1 2026 revenue 15.56B, but no segment breakdown for PV inverters and ESS separately. Sell-side estimates imply ESS profit ~120B in 2027, but this is not official.
Multiple KPE sources discuss US policy risk with different probability assessments. Resolving requires official FCC/FERC action.
Hydrogen business mentioned as 'stable expansion, good orders' but no figures provided; not included as a separate material segment.
SST/AIDC power segment is pre-revenue; all financial estimates are speculative. The July 9 SST product launch is a key verification gate.
PE TTM is 21.64, PB is 5.30, PS TTM is 3.01. Implied TTM earnings ~11.9B, sales ~85.7B. Market cap 258B.
Peer comparison score 61.9 (ranks 2/7). Caution: v4 score may have data distortions.
Steel cost proxy (Rebar) flat over look-back, but ESS margins more influenced by LFP battery cost and regional mix.
Gross margin vs. operating margin divergence flagged; need to decompose opex (R&D 41.75B in FY2025, finance expense ratio +2.42pp in Q1 2026).

The demand-supply causal chain for ESS is supported by multiple private channel checks (Europe growth revision, AIDC orders, CNESA bidding data), but most are proxy or private evidence. Confidence is moderate.
No baseline or unit values provided for many KPE hypotheses; they are listed as missing_inputs.

operating model driver coverage (project_backlog_delivery): delivery/acceptance, receivable/collection
